memoryArtifacts.headline: Memorial
memoryArtifacts.description:
The correct name of this person is Ida de Toëny, Countess of Norfolk (de Tosny), NOT Ida (Isabel) Plantagenet. She was married to Roger Bigod and was the mistress of King Henry II of England.

from Wi …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk於1160年之後出生在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德時，她的父親Ralph de Tosny8860歲，母親Marguerite Beaumont8874歲。她於大約1181年12月與Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1204年3月31日在英國越可爾利朋，埋葬在海地尼培斯安-亞-維奧迪特-特盧-德-尼培斯波迪。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk was born after 1160, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Ralph de Tosny, was 8860 and her mother, Marguerite Beaumont, was 8874. She married Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk about December 1181. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 31 March 1204, in Ripon, Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Body, Petit-Trou-de-Nippes, Anse-à-Veau, Nippes, Haiti.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk est née après 1160, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph de Tosny, avait 8860 ans et sa mère, Marguerite Beaumont, avait 8874 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk environ décembre 1181. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 mars 1204, à Ripon, Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Body, Petit-Trou-de-Nippes, Anse-à-Veau, Nip, Haïti.

ja.lifeSummary: Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolkが1160年以後にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで生まれた時，父Ralph de Tosnyは8860歳，母Marguerite Beaumontは8874歳であった。約1181年12月にRoger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1204年3月31日にイギリスヨークシュアイアリプオンで死去し，ハイチニップスアンセ-a-ベアウペティト-トロウ-デ-ニップスボディにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk(이)가 1160년 이후 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph de Tosny(은)는 8860세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marguerite Beaumont(은)는 8874세였다. 그녀는 대략 1181년 12월 Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 3월 31일 영국 요르크실르 리폰에서 사망했으며 아이티 니프스 앤세-a-브이 페티트-트루-데-니프스 보디에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk nasceu após 1160, em Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph de Tosny, tinha 8860 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite Beaumont, tinha 8874 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk aproximadamente dezembro de 1181. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 31 de março de 1204, em Ripon, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Body, Petit-Trou-de-Nippes, Anse-à-Veau, Nip, Haiti.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida de Tosny Countess of Norfolk родилась после 1160 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Ralph de Tosny было 8860 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite Beaumont, 8874. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Bigod 3rd Earl of Norfolk and Suffolk около декабрь 1181 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 31 марта 1204 г. в местности рипон, еркшир, Англия и была похоронена в местности боди, петит-троу-де-ниппесь, ансе-a-во, ниппесь, Гаити.
